narration: Whenever you see three pricing tiers, one of them is fake. It's called the decoy effect, and MIT proved it can nearly triple how many people choose the most expensive option.
visual: Dark background. Three pricing tier boxes spring in: BASIC $9, STANDARD $18 (red border, DECOY label), PREMIUM $20 (amber glow). Title THE DECOY EFFECT in amber.

narration: Dan Ariely tested this at MIT using The Economist. Web-only was $59, print-only was $125, and web plus print was also $125 — who'd ever pay the same price for less?
visual: Light background. Three subscription option cards stack in: Web-only $59 (green), Print-only $125 (red, big X animates over it), Web+Print $125 (amber, 'Best value!' label).

narration: Without the decoy, only 32 percent chose the expensive bundle. Add that useless print-only option back in and 84 percent picked it. One fake price nearly tripled premium sales.
visual: Dark background. Two bar charts grow upward side by side: left bar gray '32% — No decoy', right bar amber '84% — With decoy'. Label 'Nearly 3x' appears with an upward arrow.

narration: This trap is everywhere — streaming services, gym memberships, coffee sizes, restaurant menus. Wherever there are three options, the middle one is almost always there to push you toward the top.
visual: Light background. Three service examples appear as cards: Streaming (Basic/Standard/Premium), Gym (Silver/Gold/Platinum), Coffee (Small/Medium/Large). Middle tier highlighted amber as 'DECOY' on each.

narration: The average American makes 35 decoy-influenced purchases a year, overspending $80 per trigger. That's $2,800 every year — drained by a price that was never meant to be chosen.
visual: Dark background. Piggy bank SVG. Dollar counter animates from $0 to $2,800 in amber. Then '$112,000 invested over 40 years' springs in red below.

narration: Your defense: ignore the middle option entirely. Compare only the cheapest and most expensive — does the jump justify the cost? That two-second habit saves you $2,800 every single year.
visual: Light background. Shield SVG with animated checkmark. Two steps spring in: 1) Skip the middle tier. 2) Compare cheapest vs. priciest only. CTA: follow for more money psychology.
